cooperates.cooperate_desc: Aamon is quite squishy and relies on staying in the fight to cycle his daggers. Floryn is the perfect partner because her Lantern of Hope gives Aamon extra stats (Magic Power and HP) without taking up an item slot. Furthermore, her global ultimate provides instant heals and a shield, ensuring Aamon survives CC or heavy poke while he is camouflaged.

Aamon thrives when enemies are grouped up and stationary. Tigreal's Implosion ultimate can pull multiple enemies into one spot and stun them, allowing Aamon to throw his daggers and unleash his Endless Shards ultimate for massive area-of-effect burst damage.

counters.counter_desc: He is a nightmare for Aamon because of his Divine Judgment ultimate. This suppression skill is "point-and-click," meaning it can lock Aamon down even if he is moving fast. Once suppressed, Aamon cannot go invisible or use his skills, making him an easy target for the enemy team.

Similar to Kaja, Franco provides a massive threat with his Bloody Hunt suppression. If Franco lands a hook or simply flickers in to use his ultimate, Aamon's mobility and camouflage are completely negated. Because Aamon has low defensive stats, being held in place for even two seconds usually results in an instant trip back to the base.

dominants.dominant_desc: In the early game, Aamon's primary goal is to reach Level 4 as quickly as possible to unlock his ultimate. Focus on a fast jungle clear and use his camouflage state to rotate between lanes undetected. Look for overextended enemies, especially squishy marksmen or mages, and use the Ice Retribution to slow them down before unleashing your combo. It is vital to secure the first Turtle to give your team a gold advantage and boost your own damage. Remember that Aamon's strength lies in his "hit and run" tactics; hit your skills to trigger invisibility, reposition to avoid return fire, and only commit to the kill once you have enough shards on the ground for a maximum damage ultimate.

emblems.emblem_desc: The Mage Emblem set is chosen to maximize Aamon's burst potential by providing essential Magic Power and Magic Penetration. Rupture in the first tier adds extra penetration to ensure his daggers shred through the enemy's early-game defense. Bargain Hunter is vital for a jungler as it allows him to buy core equipment at a 5% discount, letting him reach his "one-shot" power spike much faster than his opponent. Finally, Lethal Ignition is the perfect finishing touch; since Aamon hits multiple times with his daggers and ultimate, this talent easily triggers a scorch effect for extra damage, ensuring that high-priority targets don't escape with a sliver of health

The Assassin Emblem is chosen for its superior Adaptive Penetration and Movement Speed, which are vital for Aamon to navigate the jungle and secure kills. Swift is selected in the first tier to boost his attack speed, making his enhanced basic attacks feel much smoother and increasing his damage output while in camouflage. Wilderness Blessing is a key pick for a jungler because it increases his movement speed in the river and jungle areas, allowing for incredibly fast rotations and ganks. Finally, Impure Rage provides extra adaptive damage based on the target's max HP while also restoring mana, ensuring Aamon can stay in the field longer without having to return to base.

equips.equip_desc: This build is designed to provide Aamon with a lethal mix of high-burst magic damage and late-game durability. Ice Hunter's Arcane Boots and Genius Wand are essential for early magic penetration, ensuring your daggers shred through the enemy team's initial defenses. Sky Piercer is a game-changer for finishing off low-health targets, while Holy Crystal and Blood Wings provide a massive boost to your total Magic Power and a significant shield for extra protection. Finally, Oracle is included to enhance Aamon's natural HP regen and shield effects, though you can easily switch it with Divine Glaive if you need to pierce through heavy magic defense, or Feather of Heaven if you prefer a playstyle that focuses more on enhanced basic attacks.

hero.hero_overview: Aamon is a high-burst Magic Assassin who excels at picking off backline targets and disappearing into thin air. Unlike most assassins who rely on pure mobility, Aamon utilizes his invisible state to reposition and mitigate damage.
hero.hero_strength:
Insane Single-Target Burst: His Ultimate, Endless Shards, can one-shot squishy heroes if you've accumulated enough shards on the ground.

Fast Farming: Thanks to his enhanced basic attacks, he clears jungle creeps quickly, allowing him to hit level 4 and start ganking before the enemy jungler.

Snowball Potential: Once Aamon gets a lead, he can dominate the mid-game, ending fights before the enemy can even react.
hero.hero_weakness:
Weak Against Hard CC: Aamon's survivability relies entirely on his movement speed and camouflage, If he gets hit by a "suppress" or a long stun (like Francos Ultimate or Kaja), he's incredibly squishy and can be taken down instantly.

Highly Dependent on Skill Accuracy: His damage and invisibility are triggered by landing skills. If you miss your daggers, you lose your camouflage and your enhanced basic attacks, leaving you vulnerable with no escape.

Late-Game Falloff: While he dominates the mid-game, he can struggle in the ultra-late game when enemies start building Athenas Shield or Radiant Armor. Once the enemy carries have defensive items, his "one-shot" potential drops significantly.

hero.hero_overview: Aamon is a magic assassin specializing in stealth and burst damage. His passive skill allows him to enter a camouflage state after every skill hit, making him untargetable by basic attacks and targeted skills. His main role is fast jungle clearing and quickly taking out key squishy targets in the mid and late game.
hero.hero_strength:
High Burst Damage: His ultimate can instantly delete marksmen and mages when shards are accumulated.
Stealth and Survivability: Constant camouflage grants extra movement speed and restores HP, allowing for easy escapes.
No Mana Consumption: All skills are limited only by cooldowns, enabling constant pressure.
Fast Farming: Clears jungle camps efficiently thanks to enhanced basic attacks after using skills.
hero.hero_weakness:
Weak Early Game: Low damage and long cooldowns before getting his ultimate and core items.
Vulnerability to CC: Any hard crowd control breaks his camouflage and makes him an easy target due to low HP.
Skill Accuracy Dependent: Missing his second skill prevents instant camouflage, leaving him defenseless.
Countered by Reveal: Heroes capable of seeing invisible enemies completely negate his main advantage.

spell.spell_desc:
Ice Retribution (Blue) — Standard Choice (80% of cases)
Essential for increasing movement speed in combination with your passive skill. It allows you to effectively chase down targets or quickly reposition while in camouflage.
Flame Retribution (Red) — Situational Choice
Pick this against high-mobility heroes like Joy, Fanny, or Ling. Since these heroes aren't naturally a major threat to Aamon's mechanics, the Red Smite helps reduce their damage and increase your own in direct duels, compensating for the lack of extra movement speed from the Blue Smite.

dominants.dominant_desc: In the early game maximize aamon's passive, do not go trading basic attacks. Use your skills and regen for the full duration while stacking your passive completely. Then do 1-2 basic attacks and camouflage immediately with a skill. use 1 skill at a time and never waste 2 skills at once. Use basic attacks only when your passive is full.

Get as many kills as possible, ks is okay if you are using sky piercer as you need the stacks to scale into the late game. Position properly and control the map with wave pressure and your absense. Go from the angles they least expect and appear when they are low hp

Vision is your enemy. Always have your frontliners check bushes, or use your second skill to check bushes. Read enemy positions constantly. Prepare for item swaps, and replace sky piercer with divine glaive if your stacks are below 20

dominants.dominant_desc: During the initial phase, Aamon must focus on securing a level advantage by starting the rotation at the Monster Buff on the Turtle side. This specific pathing ensures that the buff respawns exactly as the Turtle appears at the two-minute mark, providing the fresh Mana and Cooldown Reduction needed to spam shards and secure the objective. Avoid overextending before Level 4; instead, use the camouflage from your passive to scout enemy positions and perform quick, low-risk pokes. Success in this phase is defined by efficient farming and reaching your first core power spike to begin backline infiltration.

When facing heavy early-game disruption or an aggressive enemy jungle invade, focus on safe farming and objective avoidance to mitigate gold loss. Utilize the camouflage from your passive to scout safely and secure peripheral jungle camps while waiting for the enemy to overextend. As you reach the mid-game, prioritize picking off isolated low-health targets to rebuild momentum through the execution threshold of your core items. By maintaining tactical patience and using your hit-and-run loop to secure assists or kills, you can recover your economy and transition into a dominant late-game threat.

As the match progresses, Aamon transitions into a high-value executioner role. Utilize the camouflage loop to bypass the enemy frontline and target vulnerable Mages or Marksmen during team fights. Always initiate with a skill to trigger invisibility, reposition for a high-shard Endless Blades ultimate, and immediately vanish back into stealth to disengage. Focus on map macro by pressuring lanes and securing the Lord, ensuring you always keep your camouflage active to remain untargetable while navigating dangerous territory. Tactical patience is key; wait for enemy Crowd Control skills to be on cooldown before committing to a full burst rotation.

emblems.emblem_desc: The Custom Mage Emblem is the optimal choice for Aamon Jungle, providing necessary Magic Penetration to maximize burst potential. Selecting the Agility talent increases movement speed for faster map rotations, while Seasoned Hunter grants a damage bonus against the Turtle and Lord to secure neutral objectives. The core talent, Killing Spree, provides health restoration and a speed boost upon securing an elimination, allowing Aamon to survive or disengage after a successful execution. This talent combination ensures Aamon remains a mobile and durable threat capable of maintaining early-game momentum through objective control and consistent gank pressure.

The Custom Mage Emblem with the Killing Spree core talent is designed for aggressive play and high-risk eliminations. In this set, Swift provides increased Attack Speed to facilitate faster shard generation through enhanced basic attacks, while Bargain Hunter accelerates Aamon's item progression by reducing equipment costs.

The Custom Mage Emblem utilizing Quantum Charge focuses on sustained kiting and consistent health management. This configuration uses Swift to improve attack fluidity and Bargain Hunter to reach core power spikes more economically. The core talent, Quantum Charge, grants a 30% movement speed increase for 1.5 seconds and restores 75-180 HP whenever damage is dealt with basic attacks. For Aamon, this creates a powerful synergy with his enhanced basic attacks, allowing him to maintain high health while farming and providing the necessary mobility to kite enemies or chase down targets without relying solely on his camouflage.

equips.equip_desc: This configuration is optimized for high-tempo matches where Aamon can capitalize on early-game momentum to dominate fragile targets. By focusing on maximum burst damage and execution thresholds, this set ensures that infiltration attempts result in confirmed kills to snowball a gold lead. The primary utility of this build is to force enemy carries out of the game through overwhelming power, while the hybrid stats ensure his passive-enhanced basic attacks remain lethal throughout the match.

Designed for matches where Aamon faces heavy Physical damage or persistent Crowd Control, this set prioritizes durability without sacrificing offensive utility. Brute Force Breastplate replaces the early luxury item to provide physical defense, extra attack power, and much-needed Resilience to reduce debuff durations. This allows Aamon to survive initial bursts and maintain stacks in high-pressure team fights. The transition into Holy Crystal and Rose Gold Meteor ensures that even with a defensive posture, Aamon retains the hybrid scaling and shield capacity required to execute enemies and disengage safely.

This set is the optimal choice when Aamon performs well early but encounters enemies who are actively stacking Magic Defense. By retaining Sky Piercer, Aamon keeps his execution pressure high, while the late-game addition of Brute Force Breastplate provides the bulk necessary to survive late-game physical threats. The inclusion of Divine Glaive as a core component ensures that his shards and ultimate can pierce through high-magic-resistance targets, making this build the most balanced for consistent damage output against diverse enemy compositions.

hero.hero_overview: Aamon is a Magic Assassin utilized in the Jungler role who excels at high-tempo map pressure and backline execution. His gameplay centers on a hit-and-run strategy using shard manipulation and camouflage. Unlike traditional assassins who rely on dashes, Aamon uses skill-triggered invisibility from his passive to navigate the battlefield untargeted and reposition for kills. His ultimate serves as a primary execution tool that scales damage based on the number of shards on the ground and the target's missing health.
hero.hero_strength: Aamon dominates through extreme burst damage and superior map mobility. His core power is Invisible Armor, a passive providing untargetability, health sustain, and high movement speed to bypass frontliners. He excels at rotating rapidly to punish overextended squishy heroes. In combat, his Enhanced Basic Attacks utilize hybrid scaling to deal massive damage and reduce skill cooldowns. By stacking shards through aggressive micro-play, Aamon can trigger his Endless Blades ultimate to execute low-health enemies with high-velocity precision.
hero.hero_weakness: Aamon is primarily countered by persistent Crowd Control and Continuous Damage, both of which lock him out of his survival loop by instantly canceling his Invisible Armor. Because his camouflage is disabled upon taking any damage, low-interval damage sources reveal his location, negate his natural healing, and leave his low durability exposed. He is significantly hindered by the Vengeance Battle Spell, which reflects shard damage from his Ultimate back to him, immediately breaking the stealth he would normally gain after casting. Furthermore, Aamon is vulnerable to Burst Heroes who can eliminate him before he can disengage, as well as Magic Defense and Tanky Heroes who survive his execution threshold. His early-game momentum is also fragile; he is susceptible to Bully Heroes who perform aggressive Jungle Invades, stealing monsters to create a gold and experience gap that prevents him from scaling effectively.

data.recommend: This guide offers tactical insights for Aamon, focusing on jungle pathing and shard management. Use situational builds and counter-strategies to dominate matches and secure high-tier victories.

spell.spell_desc: Ice Retribution is the mandatory battle spell for Aamon in the Jungler role, establishing him as the team core for objective control. This spell provides a passive benefit that increases damage to jungle monsters for a faster farming rotation and grants dominance over the Turtle and Lord. Using the Ice Hunter blessing from the blue jungle boots is essential for Aamon, as it allows him to steal movement speed from enemy heroes to enhance his agility and kill potential. To maximize the spell, prioritize stacking its power by securing five kills or assists against monsters or heroes to unlock the ability to cast it on enemies. Tactically, it is vital to avoid using Retribution carelessly; always reserve the spell for Turtle and Lord contests to ensure the true damage execution secures these critical objectives for the team.

dominants.dominant_desc:
In early game, you need to upgrade your 2nd skill first, then your  1st skill. The skill upgrade priorities should be 3rd skill, 1st skill, and 2nd skill. Note that you only need 2nd skill to check bushes and for safe distance camouflage.
Always max your stacks as much as possible, you can use jungle creeps to maximize stacks faster, but be careful that they may hit you and disrupt your camouflage.

When dealing with sustained heroes, just play with them by using your passive to hit them, make sure that you can dodge their skills, even with multiple enemies. If you can't dodge them, just run away, specially if they are skilled.

You can use wilderness blessing if the terrain is expanding river.

Good against sustain heroes and with defensive items
You can change sky piercer with holy crystal if you have no stack in mid games

hero.hero_overview:
Buy rapid boots as soon as possible so that you can take a peek to enemy's jungle and expose as many enemies as you can. After you bought feather of heaven as 1st item, you can now kill squishy heroes. Then sky piercer, genius/glowing wand, holy crystal, blood wings (or you can use bothe genius or glowing wand).
Make sure to always keep your stacks full before engaging the enemy to achieve higher damage.

hero.hero_overview:
Role: Assassin/Mage
 
Specialties: Burst Damage, Stealth, Reap
 
Difficulty: High
 
Here's a breakdown of his key aspects:
 
- Gameplay Style: Aamon is a burst damage assassin who excels at ambushing enemies and quickly eliminating them before they can react. He relies heavily on his invisibility to position himself and surprise his targets. He's a high-risk, high-reward hero.

- Skills:

- Passive - Invisible Armor: Aamon enters Camouflage when not dealing or taking damage for a few seconds. While Camouflaged, he gains increased Movement Speed and recovers HP. His next Basic Attack or Skill deals extra damage and marks the target. This is what makes Aamon so slippery and dangerous.

- Skill 1 - Soul Shards: Aamon throws multiple daggers at nearby enemies, dealing damage. This is his main source of poke and damage in team fights.

- Skill 2 - Slayer Shards: Aamon throws a dagger that bounces between enemies, dealing damage and slowing them. This is good for slowing down targets and setting up kills.

- Ultimate - Endless Blades: Aamon throws multiple blades at a target, dealing massive damage. This is his main burst damage tool and is used to finish off enemies.
hero.hero_strength:
- Strengths:

- High Burst Damage: He can quickly eliminate squishy targets.

- Invisibility: Allows him to ambush enemies and escape from dangerous situations.

- Excellent Mobility: His passive and skills give him good mobility.

- Good Ganker: He can easily roam around the map and gank lanes
hero.hero_weakness:
- Crowd Control (CC): Aamon is very vulnerable to CC effects like stuns, knock-ups, and slows. If he's hit with CC, he'll have a hard time escaping or dealing damage. Heroes with good CC skills like Aurora, Eudora, or Chou can be effective counters.

- High Burst Damage: While Aamon has invisibility, he's not immune to damage. Heroes with high burst damage like Aldous or Eudora can take him out quickly before he can even disappear or deal significant damage.

- Heroes Who Can Detect Invisible Units: Some heroes have skills that can detect invisible heroes, like Hanzo with Ninjutsu: Demon Feast or Lesley with her Tactical Grenade. The presence of these heroes will make it difficult for Aamon to ambush or escape.

- Weak Early Game: Aamon usually needs some core items to become strong. In the early game, he is relatively weak and easy to defeat. Take advantage of this phase to pressure Aamon and prevent him from farming.

- Skill Dependent: Aamon relies heavily on his skills to deal damage and survive. If his skills are on cooldown, he becomes an easy target. Watch Aamon's skill cooldowns and attack him when his skills are unavailable.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Aamon and Floryn have good synergy because Floryn provides essential healing and shields to Aamon, a hero who lacks mobility and survivability, allowing him to stay in the fight longer to execute his combo and secure kills. Floryn can also protect Aamon by controlling enemies with her ultimate and healing him during his invisibility, making them a strong duo to take down high-priority targets.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Consistently use your skills to stay in camouflage, gaining mobility to reposition, chase enemies, or escape danger. 
Hitting minions or jungle creeps with skills or enhanced basic attacks helps build armor charges faster, allowing you to unleash your full ultimate combo for massive damage. 
Aamon is effective at assassinating vulnerable marksmen and mages due to his burst damage and ability to sneak up on enemies. 
When low on health, spam your skills to enter camouflage and regenerate HP, allowing you to stay on the battlefield instead of returning to base. 
his enhanced basic attacks between skills for maximum damage output.
